---
title: "*Resistance is Futile. Entropy Always Wins* (Introduction to the Second Law of Thermodynamics) Reference Page"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
::: {.callout-important title="Still Under Construction!"}
This web page is just a bare outline. The complete details have not been filled in yet.
:::
# Intro to *Resistance is Futile. Entropy Always Wins*

Main title explanation

## Resistance is Futile. Entropy Always Wins

Video 1 explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/yqVUz1WA7M4?si=9JXVNDB8bsxZmPqH" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

## Definitions

[The Second Law of Thermodynamics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Second_law_of_thermodynamics)
: No apparatus can operate in such a way that its only effect (in system and surroundings) is to convert heat absorbed by a system completely into work,<br/>**or**<br/>It is impossible by a cyclic process to convert the heat absorbed by a system completely into work,<br/>**or**<br/>No process is possible which consists solely in the transfer of heat from one temperature level to a higher one.

[Heat Engine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Heat_engine)
: A (usually cyclic) system that transforms thermal energy or heat into work.

Heat-Engine or Thermal Efficiency, $\eta$
: The *thermal efficiency* or *heat-engine efficiency* is the ratio of the work produced by a heat engine to the heat extracted from the high-temperature reservoir powering the heat engine. With absolute signs, the definition is $\eta \equiv |W|/|Q_\mathrm{hot}|$

[Isothermal Heat Reservoir](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thermal_reservoir)
: A reservoir that can accept or reject heat at a constant temperature. Any real reservoir has a finite capacity to accept or reject heat. An example would be a well-mixed container of liquid water and ice at atmospheric pressure. It will remain at $0\ ^\circ\mathrm{C}$ during heat addition until the ice is fully melted, and during heat rejection until the water is fully frozen.

[Carnot Engine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Carnot_heat_engine)
: A theoretical heat engine that operates at the maximum possible thermal efficiency of $\eta = 1 - (T_\mathrm{cold})/(T_\mathrm{hot})$, where the temperature of the reservoirs are expressed as absolute temperatures.

Carnot Efficiency
: The thermal efficiency of a Carnot engine, given by $\eta = 1 - (T_\mathrm{cold})/(T_\mathrm{hot})$, where the temperature of the reservoirs are expressed as absolute temperatures.

[Entropy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Entropy)
: The degree of randomness in a system, or the heat transferred to a system divided by the absolute temperature of the transfer, $Q/T$.

# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### The video previous to the previous video (i.e., two videos back)

**React Hard: The First Law Gets Chemical**<br/>*Example of the 1^st^ Law for Chemical Reactions*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/q-W5Y1d85go), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/Example1stLawForReactingSystemsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/111-EnergeticsOfReactionsPt1Visuals.qmd)

### The previous video (i.e., one video back)

**React Harder: Formation Heats and Alternate Endings**<br/>*2 more Examples of the 1^st^ Law for Chemical Reactions*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/iqxJP6g-Qsc), [Info Page](../../balances/energy/Example1stLawForReactingSystemsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/energy/112-EnergeticsOfReactionsPt2Visuals.qmd)

### The next video (i.e., one video forward)

**The ΔS-sentials of Calculating Entropy Changes**<br/>*Calculating $\Delta S$ for heat reservoirs, ideal gases, and Steam*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/z2e5Rq5r288), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/CalculatingEntropyChangesVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/115-CalcEntropyVisuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**Sadi Carnot and the Power of Fire, the 1824 Edition Part 1**<br/>*The Air-Standard Carnot Cycle*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/EDJQAvBIAok), [Info Page](../../balances/entropy/TheCarnotCycleVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../balances/entropy/116-Visuals.qmd)
